definition.title: Learn Siva! Samoan Dance Workshop
definition.description:
Join us for a variety of programs featuring the diverse cultures of Polynesia, Melanesia, and Micronesia, including Hawaiian, Samoan, Tongan, Fijian, and Cook Islander peoples. All programs are free to attend. Space and supplies are limited.

Let's learn Siva Samoa! Participants will learn graceful, expressive hand gestures to Samoan melodies. For children ages 5+ and their families. 

Generously sponsored by the Cupertino Library Foundation. 

For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

Join us in the Children's Activity Room for family fun with your little one! Stories, music, movement, and more!
Storytimes are bilingual (Spanish/English) and all ages are welcome to attend.

¡Acompáñenos en la sala de actividades para niños para divertirse en familia con su pequeño! ¡Cuentos, música, movimiento y más!
Las horas de cuentos son bilingües (español/inglés) y todas las edades son bienvenidas.


For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.
definition.title: Bilingual Spanish & English Family Storytime
Maracas & Movement
definition.description:
Get ready to wiggle, giggle, and shout “¡Vamos a bailar!” as we explore music, movement, and children’s rhymes in English and Spanish. Families and caregivers are invited to jump into the fun with percussion instruments, scarves, shakers, and more.

Prepárate para moverte, reír y gritar “¡Vamos a bailar!” mientras exploramos música, movimiento y rimas infantiles en inglés y español. Los niños y sus familias están invitados a unirse a la diversión con instrumentos de percusión, pañuelos, maracas y más.

definition.title: Tai Chi Class for Adults
definition.description:
Find balance and calm through Tai Chi. Join Tai Chi instructor, Lawrence Kwock, in a fun and welcoming environment. Focus your mind, visualize movement and connect within. All levels welcome

definition.title: Summer 2026 Plant a Seed, Read: Cascada de Flores
definition.description:
Join us for some joyful bilingual music and movement time with Cascada de Flores! Cascada de Flores transports audiences on a joyous journey through the heart of Latin America. With a captivating blend of traditional Mexican and Caribbean rhythms, their bilingual performances are a delightful fusion of music, storytelling, and dance. Learn or practice Spanish with songs, stories, dancing, puppets, and a lot of imagination!

This is a special event for this year's Summer Reading Program: Plant a Seed, Read.

For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+ days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.

definition.title: 2026 Summer Reading Program: Learning Chinese language and culture
definition.description:
Join us for an interactive program designed to help participants learn basic Chinese language skills and develop an appreciation for Chinese culture through fun and engaging activities. All materials will be provided. This program focuses on traditional Chinese.

The program runs for four weeks, meeting twice per week, with two‑hour sessions. 

This is a free program for ADULTS ONLY. Space is limited to 20 people, and REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED for each session. 

The class schedule is as follows

Tuesdays: 6:30-8:30 on June 2, 9, 16, 23

Wednesday: 6:30-8:30 on 6/10

Saturdays: 10:30-12:30 on 6/6, 6/20, 6/27

This program is offered in partnership between TECO (Education Division of the Taipei Economic and Cultural Office).and Campbell Library

Classes will be taught by Linda Chen, a Mandarin teacher at Branham High School with over 20 years of experience teaching Mandarin to both high school students and adults.

definition.title: Friends of the Campbell Library's Jewelry Sale
definition.description:
Stop by the Friends of the Campbell Library's jewelry sale, and choose from a wide variety of interesting items! Whether you're looking for fine or costume jewelry; vintage or modern, there will be something for every taste! Pay by cash or card - all purchases hep support our library.

Free admission, and free parking. Sign up to hear about future fundraising events at the Friends of the Campbell Library's website: Sales - Friends of the Campbell Library

Filipino American puppeteer Ramon Abad presents "Tito Ramon's Pop-Up Puppet Show". This is a family-friendly exploration of modern puppetry that goes beyond Sesame Street! Kids will be invited to come up to the stage for some fun audience participation with puppets, too!

definition.title: Summer 2026 Plant a Seed, Read: Happy Birds
definition.description:
Join us as we welcome Happy Birds to the Milpitas Library! Be prepared to be amazed as the Happy Birds perform tricks including riding a bike, singing songs, and playing basketball. These famous feathered friends have been featured on The Tonight Show and The Ellen Show and should not be missed! Happy Birds are sure to have you flapping your wings with delight!

This is a special event for this year's Summer Reading Program: Plant a Seed, Read.

Tickets are required for entry. Pick up a FREE ticket 30 minutes before the performance. Attendees must be present to collect their tickets. *(Limit of two adults per family.)*

definition.title: Summer 2026 Plant a Seed, Read: Beginning Mandarin for Kids
definition.description:
3rd - 8th graders: Interested in learning Mandarin? Join us this summer to learn the basics of this language spoken by over 1 billion speakers in the world. You'll learn basic sounds, words and phrases. No experience necessary.

Registration required. Please register using the student(s) name. Registration is for the entire series. Registration will open May 1st.

Classes will be on:

  • Mondays, June 8, 15, 22, 29 and July 6
  • Saturdays, June 13, 20 and 27th

This program is graciously sponsored by the Taipei Economic and Cultural Office.

definition.title: Adult & Teen Crafternoon: Tea Cup Bouquet
definition.description:
The Adult & Teen Crafternoon occurs every month on the third Saturday, from 3 - 4:30 PM. Patrons ages 13+ are invited to take part in a different craft or art project each month. You are welcome to drop-in at any time, but please note most projects will take over 30 minutes to complete. We hope to see you there!

June's craft celebrates our Summer Reading Theme of Plant a Seed, Read! The craft? Tea Cup Bouquets! Make tissue paper flowers to fill a (surprisingly fancy but still paper) tea cup. It's also the perfect time to make a bouquet in the Pride flag colors of your choice!

No registration is required; walk-in while supplies last.

The program space is for adults and teens (ages 13+) only. Please browse children's events if you are interested in events for younger ages.

definition.locationDetails: upstairs Community room and Quiet Study B
definition.description:
Learn how to use a sewing machine and sew your own fabric roll-up organizer or bring in your own project to work on. Sewing machines and materials to make one organizer will be provided per person. This event is for adults and teens.

Please visit the Gilroy Library and register in person at the upstairs Information desk starting June 1. We will not take registration by phone or email. Register for either the session on Saturday, June 20 (2:30-4:30pm) or Monday, June 22 (6:30-8:30pm). Please arrive on time otherwise your spot may be given to someone on the Waitlist.

Led by Marsi O'Malley-Riley, a retired Gilroy librarian. She currently volunteers at Fabmo, a non-profit organization that rescues designer samples and fabric and makes them available to the public for creative reuse.

definition.locationDetails: Program Room (previously the Computer Training Center)
definition.description:
The Adult & Teen Crafternoon occurs every month on the third Saturday, from 3 - 4:30 PM. Patrons ages 13+ are invited to take part in a different craft or art project each month. You are welcome to drop-in at any time, but please note most projects will take over 30 minutes to complete. We hope to see you there!

This event page will be updated with the exact craft by the 1st of the month, so please check back closer to the program date. 

No registration is required; walk-in while supplies last.

The program space is for adults and teens (ages 13+) only. Please browse children's events if you are interested in events for younger ages.

For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@lib.sccgov.org.
definition.title: Adult & Teen Crafternoon

override.title: Adult & Teen Crafternoon: Acrylic Coloring
override.description:
The Adult & Teen Crafternoon occurs every month on the third Saturday, from 3 - 4:30 PM. Patrons ages 13+ are invited to take part in a different craft or art project each month. You are welcome to drop-in at any time, but please note most projects will take over 30 minutes to complete. We hope to see you there!

January's craft will be Acrylic Coloring!

We will be using canvas and acrylic paint for our materials, with a range of options for both beginners and experienced artists. If you would like a relaxing afternoon of coloring-in with paint, you can use the provided transfer paper to trace a pre-made design onto your canvas; or, you can create your own hand-drawn masterpiece! This activity is self-paced, and while staff will be present for guidance it is not a formal class.

No registration is required; walk-in while supplies last.

The program space is for adults and teens (ages 13+) only. Please browse children's events if you are interested in events for younger ages.

For accessibility accommodations (ex. ASL), please fill out the online form at sccld.org/accessibility 7+days prior to the event. If you need assistance with the form, speak with a staff member at your local library, call (408) 540-3945, or email us at ask@sccl.org.

definition.description:
Join us for an interactive program designed to help participants learn basic Chinese language skills and develop an appreciation for Chinese culture through fun and engaging activities. All materials will be provided. This program focuses on traditional Chinese.

The program runs for four weeks, meeting twice per week, with two‑hour sessions. Participants MUST COMMIT TO ATTENDING ALL SESSIONS. 

This is a free program for ADULTS ONLY. Space is limited, and REGISTRATION IS REQUIRED.

The class schedule is as follows

Tuesdays: 6:30-8:30 on June 2, 9, 16, 23

Wednesday: 6:30-8:30 on 6/10

Saturdays: 10:30-12:30 on 6/6, 6/20, 6/27

This program is offered in partnership between TECO (Education Division of the Taipei Economic and Cultural Office).and Campbell Library

Classes will be taught by Linda Chen, a Mandarin teacher at Branham High School with over 20 years of experience teaching Mandarin to both high school students and adults.

SA.directions: To visit Saratoga Library, find driving directions and public transportation options.
SA.meetingRooms: You must have a valid SCCLD library card to book a room, as well as read and agree to adhere to any room usage requirements for the room you are booking. To find out more about this service, please refer to our Book a Room @ the Library page.
SA.accessibilityInfo: The Santa Clara County Library District provides a variety of Accessibility Services to assist our community. To find out more about these services, please refer to our Accessibility Services page.

WO.name: Woodland Branch Library
WO.description: Branch library of the Los Altos Library, member of the Santa Clara County Library District.
WO.directions: To visit Woodland Library, find driving directions and public transportation options.
WO.meetingRooms: You must have a valid SCCLD library card to book a room, as well as read and agree to adhere to any room usage requirements for the room you are booking. To find out more about this service, please refer to our Book a Room @ the Library page.
WO.accessibilityInfo:
The Santa Clara County Library District provides a variety of Accessibility Services to assist our community. To find out more about these services, please refer to our Accessibility Services page.

Sign up for Open Access and enjoy exclusive, self-service access to browse books, check out library materials, relax and read, or use the WiFi or our computers, printers, and copy machines. Visit Woodland Library during regular open hours to learn more!
